name: intentkit
description: IntentKit is an open-source, self-hosted cloud agent cluster that manages a collaborative team of AI agents for you.

commit.message:
delete: remove @skill pattern mechanism and explain_prompt

The @skill:category:name prompt translation is no longer needed.
Removes explain_prompt, get_skill_name_by_config, and _SKILL_CONFIG_NAMES registry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add copy-as-markdown button, create_post card attachment, and smart card link targets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove skills.csv and simplify skill pricing

Remove the Skill/SkillTable models, skills.csv (167 entries), and the
CSV-based pricing/revenue-sharing system. Replace with a `price` field
on IntentKitSkill and a global price registry built at startup.

- Add `price: Decimal` field to IntentKitSkill (default=1)
- Add global registry (build_skill_prices, get_skill_price) in skills/base.py
- Set non-default prices on ~50 skill classes extracted from CSV
- Remove dev fee/author revenue sharing from credit system
- Simplify skill_cost() to accept price directly instead of DB lookup
- Remove Skill.get_all() and price_level schema injection from agent schema
- Remove /metadata/skills endpoint
- Remove fee_dev_percentage from PaymentSettings
- Remove SELF_KEY discount type
- Delete skills.csv and generate_skill_csv.py
- Update all callers and tests (284 tests pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add ui skill group with show_card and ask_user skills

Add new ui skill category for structured UI output (cards, choices).
Backend skills return content_and_artifact tuples with CARD/CHOICE
attachment types. Frontend renders cards with optional image/label/url
and clickable choice options that auto-send user messages.

- Add ui_show_card and ui_ask_user skills with schema and icon
- Register ui skills in skills.csv
- Fix xmtp skills to include lead_text in attachments
- Exclude ui tools from ClearToolUsesEdit context clearing
- Update frontend types and render card/choice attachments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.23

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve controlled/uncontrolled input warning in CheckboxWidget

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: dynamically filter wallet_provider enum based on config

Build wallet_provider options in get_json_schema based on available
config values: none/native always present, cdp if CDP keys configured,
privy if Privy credentials set, safe if Privy + master wallet key set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add enable_activity and enable_post flags to control system skill loading

Add two optional bool flags to agent model that control whether activity
skills (create activity, recent activities) and post skills (create post,
get post, recent posts) are loaded. Default None in model, True in JSON
schema. Cache system skill instances at module level for efficiency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add recent_posts and get_post system skills

recent_posts returns post excerpts without full markdown to save LLM context.
get_post retrieves full post content by ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.22

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: advanced settings toggle, webpack polling fix, and UI improvements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: frontend hot reload in Docker by enabling Turbopack polling

Next.js 16 uses Turbopack by default, which ignores WATCHPACK_POLLING
and CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING env vars. Add watchOptions.pollIntervalMs to
next.config.ts (conditionally when WATCHPACK_POLLING is set) so
Turbopack polls for file changes inside Docker containers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: model selector widget with info panel for agent forms

Replace plain select with custom dropdown that fetches /metadata/llms,
groups models by provider, shows display names, and displays a floating
info panel on hover with rating bars (price/intelligence/speed),
context/output lengths, and capability badges (image/search).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add PancakeSwap V3 liquidity pool skills

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.20

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add agent middleware support and upgrade Node to 24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
refactor: web search and super mode

doc: add release notes for v0.11.19

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add PancakeSwap V3 swap quote and execution skills

New skill category for PancakeSwap V3 DEX with two skills:
- pancakeswap_quote: get swap quotes via QuoterV2 contract (auto-selects best fee tier)
- pancakeswap_swap: execute swaps via SmartRouter with automatic ERC20 approval

Supports BSC, Ethereum, Base, and Arbitrum chains following agent config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: separate docs build into its own workflow triggered on push

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: add release notes for v0.11.18

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: restructure docs site — flatten layout, default light theme, add introduction

- Remove nested docs/ section to eliminate double /docs/docs/ URLs
- Move all content to root level with cascade type: docs
- Add proper introduction page as site root
- Default to light theme, remove navbar theme toggle
- Sidebar sections expanded by default
- Remove edit-on-GitHub links
- Change baseURL from /docs/ to / for simpler reverse proxy setup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: shorten tool descriptions and field schemas to reduce LLM context usage

Condensed verbose tool descriptions and Field parameter descriptions across
167 skill files (~1,459 lines removed) to minimize token consumption when
tools are sent to LLMs as schemas, while preserving clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: fix docs base URL and disable language subdir redirect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
fix: override hextra rss template for hugo > 0.124 compatibility
ci: fix hugo docker build permission denied
ci: fix missing hugo docker image tag
ci: fix missing checkout step in docker job
ci: publish new images to ghcr.io

chore: release notes for v0.11.9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add multi-token Safe spending limit support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
chore: update changelog for v0.11.8 release
feat: update llm reasoning effort config and add google vertex ai support
fix: catch httpcore.ReadTimeout and add release/env to alert messages
feat: heartbeat in local docker compose
fix: tool call in local ui
fix(frontend): prevent first message from disappearing on new thread
fix: agent chat new conversation flow & chore: release v0.11.6

fix: reduce autonomous tasks heading size

refactor: store relative paths for images in DB, add CDN URL resolution at display time

- S3 client functions (store_image, store_image_bytes, store_file, store_file_bytes)
  now return relative paths instead of full CDN URLs
- Add get_cdn_url() helper in s3 client for skills to build accessible URLs
- All image/audio generation skills use get_cdn_url() when returning URLs to agent
- Twitter skills handle both CDN URLs and relative paths for image validation
- Simplify avatar.py by removing CDN prefix stripping (no longer needed)
- Frontend: add NEXT_PUBLIC_AWS_S3_CDN_URL env var and getImageUrl() utility
- Frontend: apply getImageUrl() to all agent.picture display locations
- Update Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml with new env var

releases.name: v0.11.25
releases.body:
## New Features

- **Copy as Markdown**: Post pages now include a "Copy as Markdown" button next to the title, allowing users to copy the full post content (with title) as raw Markdown.
- **Post Card in Chat**: When an agent creates a post, a clickable card is automatically sent to the user in the chat, linking directly to the post. The AI no longer needs to separately summarize or display the post.
- **`APP_BASE_URL` Config**: Added a new `APP_BASE_URL` configuration option for the frontend base URL, used for generating post links.

## Improvements

- Card links in chat now open in the current window when linking to the same site, and in a new tab for external links.
- Fixed `/post/:id` route returning 404 for dynamic post IDs.
- Cleaned up unused prompt code.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/crestalnetwork/intentkit/compare/v0.11.24...v0.11.25

releases.name: v0.11.24
releases.body:
## New Features

- **UI Skills**: Added a new UI skill group with `show_card` and `ask_user` skills, enabling agents to display rich card components and interactive prompts to users.

## Improvements

- **Simplified Skill Pricing**: Streamlined the skill pricing system by moving prices directly onto skill definitions, removing the need for a separate CSV-based configuration. This makes skill pricing more maintainable and transparent.
- **Removed Developer Fee System**: Removed the developer fee / author revenue sharing mechanism from the credit system, simplifying cost calculations for skill calls.
- Updated project dependencies.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/crestalnetwork/intentkit/compare/v0.11.23...v0.11.24

releases.name: v0.11.23
releases.body:
## What's New

- **Activity and Post Skills**: Agents can now optionally enable activity tracking and post management skills (create post, get post, recent posts) via dedicated toggle settings.
- **Dynamic Wallet Provider Options**: The wallet provider selection now automatically adapts based on your deployment configuration — only showing providers (CDP, Privy, Safe) that have the required credentials configured.

## Improvements

- Reorganized internal agent schema files for better maintainability.
- Fixed a UI warning related to checkbox inputs in the agent editor.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/crestalnetwork/intentkit/compare/v0.11.22...v0.11.23

releases.name: v0.11.22
releases.body:
## What's New

- **Model Selector Widget**: New interactive model selector with info panel for agent configuration forms, making it easier to choose and understand available LLM models.
- **Advanced Settings Toggle**: Agent form now supports collapsible advanced settings sections, keeping the UI clean while still providing access to power-user options.
- **GPT 5.4 Support**: Added support for OpenAI's latest GPT 5.4 model.

## Improvements

- Improved frontend hot reload reliability in Docker environments.
- Updated dependencies.
- Reorganized agent form field groupings for better usability.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/crestalnetwork/intentkit/compare/v0.11.21...v0.11.22

releases.name: v0.11.21
releases.body:
## New Features

- **PancakeSwap V3 Liquidity Management**: Added three new skills for managing liquidity positions on PancakeSwap V3:
  - **View Positions**: See all your V3 liquidity positions including pool details, tick ranges, uncollected fees, and farming status with pending CAKE rewards.
  - **Add Liquidity**: Create full-range liquidity positions with automatic staking into MasterChef V3 farms when eligible for CAKE rewards.
  - **Remove Liquidity**: Remove liquidity partially or fully with automatic unstaking, fee collection, CAKE reward harvesting, and NFT cleanup.
- **Linea Network Support**: PancakeSwap skills now support Linea mainnet in addition to BSC, Ethereum, Base, and Arbitrum.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/crestalnetwork/intentkit/compare/v0.11.20...v0.11.21

readme_html:
IntentKit

IntentKit is an open-source, self-hosted cloud agent cluster that manages a collaborative team of AI agents for you.

Why IntentKit

Current AI agents generally follow two paths:

  1. Local-First (e.g., OpenClaw): These serve as personal assistants or experimental tools. However, they often require expensive hardware and extensive local permissions.
  2. Cloud-Native: Similar to modern web apps, these run in the cloud, consume minimal local resources, require zero maintenance, and offer high reliability for completing tasks.

If you are looking for a robust, cloud-native solution, IntentKit is for you.

Features

  • ☁️ Cloud-Native: Ultimate resource efficiency.
  • 🤖 Collaborative AI: Multiple agents that can call and interact with each other.
  • 🔄 Out-of-the-Box Ready: Fully configured and ready to use.
  • 🔗 Crypto-Friendly: Optional Web3 and blockchain integrations.
  • 🐦 Social Media Integration: Seamlessly connects with social platforms.
  • 🛠️ Extensible Skill System: Easily add new capabilities.

Documentation

Please check out the Documentation before getting started.

Deployment

Read the Deployment Guide to get started with your setup.

Other Use Cases

Besides self-deploying IntentKit, you can also use the project in the following ways:

  • As a Python Library: Import and extend IntentKit, or use it to add agent cluster capabilities to your existing projects.
  • Via API: Regardless of the deployment method, each individual agent can expose its own API, allowing you to interact with them from external applications. See the Agent API Documentation.

Contributing

Due to the rapid pace of AI development, we currently do not accept code contributions via Pull Requests. However, submitting feature requests and reporting bugs in the GitHub Issues section is highly appreciated and the best way to contribute.

License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.
